title: Handshake vs Meet Alfred — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Meet Alfred for LinkedIn automation. See features, pricing, safety, and multi-sender support side-by-side to pick the right tool for your team.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a cloud-based LinkedIn automation platform designed from the ground up for multi-sender rotation. It lets teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included on every plan. Pricing is based on sender accounts — not per-seat — so your entire team collaborates without multiplying costs. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale to enterprise volumes.
overview.competitor: Meet Alfred is a cloud-based multichannel outreach platform that combines LinkedIn, email, and X (Twitter) automation in a single tool. Launched in 2018, it offers three plans: Basic ($59/mo per user), Pro ($99/mo per user), and Teams ($79/mo per user) when billed monthly. Annual billing drops these to approximately $29, $49, and $39 per user respectively. Meet Alfred supports Sales Navigator, includes a built-in CRM, social media post scheduling, and group/event automation. It's positioned as a multichannel solution for individuals and teams.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts automatically
featureComparison.competitor: Not available — each user manages their own LinkedIn account independently

featureComparison.handshake: Automated warmup scheduling with gradual ramp-up for new accounts
featureComparison.competitor: No dedicated warmup automation — users must manage limits manually

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox for all sender accounts — manage every conversation centrally
featureComparison.competitor: Smart LinkedIn Inbox per account — no cross-account unified view

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan, shared workspaces
featureComparison.competitor: Teams plan with team inbox management, advanced team settings, and white-label (5+ users)

featureComparison.handshake: LinkedIn-focused — email and Twitter not included
featureComparison.competitor: LinkedIn + Email + X (Twitter) automation in one platform (Pro plan and above)

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included, human-like sending patterns, cloud infrastructure
featureComparison.competitor: Cloud-based with smart delays, but no residential proxies included — uses dedicated IPs

featureComparison.handshake: Not available — focused on LinkedIn outreach automation
featureComparison.competitor: Built-in social media post scheduling across platforms

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in A/B testing on all plans
featureComparison.competitor: Message templates and variations available, but no formal A/B testing framework

featureComparison.handshake: Campaign analytics and contact management through unified dashboard
featureComparison.competitor: Built-in LinkedIn CRM with tags, notes, and contact management on all plans
LinkedIn Groups automation and Events automation on Pro plan
Zapier and webhook integrations on Pro plan

sections.handshakeWins:
Handshake's biggest advantages over Meet Alfred revolve around multi-sender rotation, pricing at scale, and account safety.

Multi-sender rotation is the fundamental differentiator. Meet Alfred treats each user as an independent operator with their own LinkedIn account and campaigns. There's no built-in way to distribute leads across multiple senders or rotate outreach automatically. Handshake does this natively — your prospects are distributed across all connected sender accounts to maximize reach while keeping each account within safe limits.

The pricing gap widens significantly as you scale. At 5 users, Meet Alfred costs $295-$495/month depending on plan tier. Handshake covers 5 senders for $199/month with unlimited team seats. At 10 users, the gap becomes $590-$990/mo vs. $299/mo — Handshake is 50-70% cheaper.

Handshake includes premium residential proxies on every plan, which is a meaningful safety advantage. Meet Alfred uses dedicated IPs but doesn't specify residential proxies — and the distinction matters significantly for LinkedIn detection avoidance.

The unified inbox across all sender accounts saves substantial time for team leads and account managers who need visibility into conversations happening across multiple LinkedIn profiles.
sections.competitorWins:
Meet Alfred's strongest card is multichannel outreach. If you need LinkedIn, email, and X (Twitter) automation in a single platform, Meet Alfred delivers all three natively from the Pro plan onwards. Handshake focuses exclusively on LinkedIn — meaning you'd need a separate tool for email outreach.

The social media post scheduling feature is unique among LinkedIn automation tools. If you're managing a company's LinkedIn content alongside outbound outreach, Meet Alfred consolidates both workflows.

Meet Alfred's LinkedIn-specific features are also broader than Handshake in some areas. Group automation lets you message LinkedIn Group members directly. Event automation targets event attendees. Content retargeting lets you create campaigns targeting people who engaged with specific posts. These are useful for warming up relationships before direct outreach.

For solo users, Meet Alfred's Basic plan at $59/mo (or $29/mo annual) is competitively priced and includes core LinkedIn automation features.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if you're scaling LinkedIn outreach across multiple sender accounts and need centralized management. It's ideal for sales teams running multi-sender rotation, agencies managing multiple client accounts, and anyone who prioritizes safe scaling with included residential proxies. If LinkedIn is your primary outbound channel and you're running 3+ sender accounts, Handshake delivers more value per dollar.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Meet Alfred if you need true multichannel outreach — LinkedIn, email, and Twitter — in a single platform. It's also a strong choice if social media post scheduling is important to your workflow, or if you need LinkedIn Group and Event automation features. For solo users who want an all-in-one multichannel tool at a single-seat price, Meet Alfred's Pro plan is compelling.
sections.verdict: Handshake wins on LinkedIn-specific outreach scaling: multi-sender rotation, unified inbox, safety, and team pricing are all clearly superior. Meet Alfred wins on multichannel breadth: LinkedIn + email + Twitter in one tool with social scheduling. If your primary goal is to scale LinkedIn outreach across multiple accounts as safely and affordably as possible, choose Handshake. If you want a single platform that covers LinkedIn, email, and social posting for individual users or small teams, Meet Alfred is a solid choice.

faqs.question: Does Meet Alfred support multi-sender rotation?
faqs.answer: No. Each Meet Alfred user manages their own LinkedIn account independently. There's no built-in way to rotate outreach across multiple accounts or distribute leads between senders. Handshake's multi-sender rotation is a core feature.

faqs.question: Is Handshake cheaper than Meet Alfred for teams?
faqs.answer: Yes, especially at scale. For 5 LinkedIn accounts, Handshake costs $199/mo vs. Meet Alfred's $295-$495/mo. For 10 accounts, it's $299/mo vs. $590-$990/mo. Handshake also includes unlimited team seats at no extra charge.

faqs.question: Can Meet Alfred do email outreach?
faqs.answer: Yes. Meet Alfred's Pro plan includes multichannel automation combining LinkedIn, email, and X (Twitter). This is a feature Handshake doesn't offer — Handshake is focused specifically on LinkedIn automation.

faqs.question: Which tool has better LinkedIn safety features?
faqs.answer: Handshake includes premium residential proxies on every plan, automated warmup scheduling, and human-like sending patterns. Meet Alfred is cloud-based with smart delays but doesn't include residential proxies. For account safety, Handshake has the edge.

faqs.question: Can I switch from Meet Alfred to Handshake?
faqs.answer: Yes. Simply connect your LinkedIn accounts to Handshake and set up new campaigns. No data migration is needed. Most teams get running within a day.
